They want to use abstrauth as the Auth Server." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="690" y="600" width="180" height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-35" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" edge="1" parent="1" source="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-32" target="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-33"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-32" value="They need to create a new Client Id. That generates a client secret.<div>This client and its id and secret are private for the organisation!&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Therefore it is given a tenantId which is the organisationId.</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="660" y="720" width="240" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-33" value="The key to this puzzle is the subscription.<div><br></div><div>Organisations subscribe to abstratium applications. Those applications use the clientId in the abstratium tenant.</div><div><br></div><div><br></div>" users only see data belonging to their organisation" <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-53" value="ms appears to allow extra roles to be assigned - see doc on gdrive" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="630" y="10" width="140" height="90" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-55" value="During initial sign-in in an abstratium app, the user is redirected to abstrauth. Abstrauth knows the clientId and hence the application. After creating the org, it can check if the client allows automatic subscription and if it does, it can create that subscription.&nbsp;<div>The JWT contains the orgId and that can be used as the tenantId in Hibernate.</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="30" y="900" width="250" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-56" value="Paid features can be based on the user having specific roles. The roles are assigned during sign-in, by using the subscription to check the contract and its billing status. If the user has credit, has prepaid or we trust them to post-pay, then the extra roles are added to the JWT and the application can trust that they may use those features." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-260" y="840" width="250" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-57" value="problems" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-140" y="130" width="130" height="40" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-58" value="solutions" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-140" y="178" width="130" height="40" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-59" value="<b><font style="font-size: 24px;">Legend</font></b>" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-128" y="88" width="60" height="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-60" value="Do we need to allow the org to unsubscribe? We could do that, as it revokes the rights of a user or organisation to an application." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="90" y="1090" width="250" height="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-61" value="The subscription should contain a list of default roles which a user is given when they first sign into an application." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-250" y="1020" width="250" height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

Owner and member.

div.div:
They need to create a new Client Id. That generates a client secret.
This client and its id and secret are private for the organisation!
Therefore it is given a tenantId which is the organisationId.

switch.text: They need to create a new Client Id. Tha...

div.div:
The key to this puzzle is the subscription.
Organisations subscribe to abstratium applications. Those applications use the clientId in the abstratium tenant.

div.div:
During initial sign-in in an abstratium app, the user is redirected to abstrauth. Abstrauth knows the clientId and hence the application. After creating the org, it can check if the client allows automatic subscription and if it does, it can create that subscription.
The JWT contains the orgId and that can be used as the tenantId in Hibernate.

switch.text: During initial sign-in in an abstratium a...

div.div: Paid features can be based on the user having specific roles. The roles are assigned during sign-in, by using the subscription to check the contract and its billing status. If the user has credit, has prepaid or we trust them to post-pay, then the extra roles are added to the JWT and the application can trust that they may use those features.
